profile.name: David
profile.role_in_household: Primary; higher earner and tends to steer investing and big-picture planning
profile.financial_literacy_level: High (tracks account balances, rates, and dates; comfortable comparing options and will correct small number errors)
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate in behavior: willing to take market risk for retirement, but wants downside scenarios and a clear rationale; dislikes leaving obviously expensive items (like high-rate debt) unattended
profile.communication_style: Structured and direct; cites specific figures and timelines, does quick math out loud, uses light filler ('so', 'right'); hedges mainly when recalling exact dates or paperwork details
emotional_tendencies: settles when there is a written plan and milestones
profile.decision_making_style: Analytical and tradeoff-based; prefers 2–3 clear choices, then decides once assumptions are explicit
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously trusting; expects transparency on taxes, fees, and implementation steps
internal_biases: anchoring to known payment amounts and interest rates
status quo bias unless benefits are quantified
hidden_concerns: pressure of being the main income engine and keeping cash flow stable
whether coverage and planning are sufficient while there is a dependent and debt
unease about high monthly outflows even if assets look strong

profile.name: Patrici
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; steadier paycheck and day-to-day budget reality-check, but often lets David lead on investment mechanics
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate (good with budgeting and goals; prefers plain English and can mix up terms like balance vs payment or premium vs benefit)
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate-to-cautious: okay with diversified investing when it feels purposeful, but becomes more conservative during volatility; values liquidity and predictability
profile.communication_style: Conversational and big-picture; speaks in round numbers ('about', 'roughly', 'I think'), asks clarifying questions, may briefly defer and then circle back with a concern later
emotional_tendencies: anxious about surprises (taxes, repairs, market drops)
relieved by simple rules and clear next steps
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-seeking; wants to understand monthly cash-flow impact and family security before agreeing, prefers incremental changes
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Warm but verifying; rapport matters, then she looks for reassurance that recommendations aren’t overly complex
hidden_concerns: retirement timing mismatch between her and David and what that means in practice
whether cash reserves feel sufficient given ongoing expenses
how illiquid/alternative holdings fit with the rest of the plan
